metadata.title: HARI — AI HR Platform Starter
metadata.description: An AI-powered HR platform starter: RBAC-gated AI tools, handbook RAG, and generative chat UI on Next.js + pgvector + Vercel AI SDK.

dashboard.signedInAs: You're signed in as {role}. What you see below is shaped by your permissions.
dashboard.assistantDescription: The assistant answers handbook questions with citations (RAG), runs HR actions through permission-checked tools, streams its reasoning, and renders rich results inline. Try one of these:
dashboard.example3: “How many vacation days do I have left, and request 2 of them next Monday.”

directory.title: Directory
directory.scopeSelf: Just your own profile — managers and HR see more.
directory.name: Name
directory.noResults: No employees match the selected filters.

timeOff.title: Time Off
timeOff.description: Tip: request time off or approve requests by asking the AI Assistant.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: The single RBAC matrix that gates the UI, server actions, and AI tools.
settings.aiToolsDescription: The assistant is only given the tools a role may use — out-of-scope tools are never injected, so the model can't attempt them.
settings.modelRegistryDescription: Chat defaults to OpenRouter free models; the Vercel AI Gateway is selectable per request. Configure keys via environment variables.
settings.localizationDescription: Currency and timezone used across the app and by the AI assistant.
settings.overviewDescription: Manage your organization, the AI assistant, and access policy.
settings.cardLocalizationDesc: Currency and timezone for the app and the assistant.
settings.cardAssistantDesc: Choose which Knowledge Base content the assistant can use.
settings.cardPermissionsDesc: The role-based permission matrix that gates every surface.
settings.cardAiToolsDesc: Which AI tools each role is given.
settings.cardModelsDesc: The chat models available to the assistant.

chat.greetingDescription: Ask about policies, your time off, the directory, or payslips.
chat.suggestion1: What is our parental leave policy?
chat.suggestion2: How many vacation days do I have left?
chat.error: Something went wrong. Check that the provider API key is set in your environment.

leave.approvalResult: {name}'s {days}-day {type} request was {status, select, APPROVED {approved} REJECTED {rejected} other {updated}}.

summary.getCurrentDateTime: The current date, time, and weekday (utility).
summary.getDateInfo: Weekday / weekend status for a given date (utility).
summary.searchHandbook: Search the employee handbook (RAG) and cite sections.
summary.getEmployeeDirectory: List employees the caller is allowed to see.
summary.requestTimeOff: Submit a time-off request for the caller.
summary.listPendingApprovals: List requests awaiting the caller's approval.
summary.approveLeave: Approve or reject a pending request.
summary.getPayslip: A payslip — own, or anyone visible with elevated rights.

login.pitchDescription: A reference implementation showing RBAC-gated AI tools, RAG over your handbook, streaming reasoning, and generative chat UI — on Next.js, Postgres + pgvector, and the Vercel AI SDK.
login.demoBuild: Demo build · pick any role to explore how permissions shape the experience.
login.chooseRole: Choose a demo role — each has different permissions.
login.passwordNote: All demo accounts use the password {password}

demo.EMPLOYEE: Sees only their own data. The AI assistant only gets self-service tools (no view of other employees, no approval rights).
demo.MANAGER: Has visibility over their direct team and can approve their leave requests.
demo.HR_ADMIN: Company-wide access to the directory, compensation, and payslips.
demo.SUPER_ADMIN: Holds all HR rights, plus access to the platform's technical settings.

kb.title: Knowledge Base
kb.description: Browse company policies and HR documents.
kb.emptyReader: No articles are available to you yet.
kb.adminDescription: Create, edit, publish and archive HR documents and collections.
kb.fieldSlugHint: Used in the URL. Leave blank to generate from the name/title.
kb.fieldContentHint: Use Heading 2 / Heading 3 for sections — each becomes a citable anchor.
kb.noCollections: No collections yet. Create one to start adding documents.
kb.documentCount: {count, plural, =0 {No documents} one {# document} other {# documents}}
kb.name: Name
kb.draftNotice: This is a draft — not visible to employees or the assistant.
kb.archivedNotice: This document is archived — not visible to employees or the assistant.
kb.deleteDocDesc: “{title}” and its search-index entries will be permanently removed. This can’t be undone.
kb.deleteCollectionDesc: “{name}” and all of its documents will be permanently removed. This can’t be undone.
kb.searchHint: Type at least 2 characters to search.
kb.slugChangeWarning: Changing the slug breaks existing links to this page.
kb.fieldImageHint: Optional decorative image shown on the collection. PNG, JPG, GIF or WebP, up to 1 MB.
kb.imageTooLarge: Image is too large (1 MB max).
kb.articleCount: {count, plural, =0 {No articles} one {# article} other {# articles}}
kb.assistantAccessDescription: Choose which Knowledge Base content the AI assistant may use when answering. This never widens access — a person's role and a document's published status still apply. Content hidden here stays readable in the Knowledge Base.
kb.assistantOverridesCount: {count, plural, =0 {No overrides} one {# override} other {# overrides}}
kb.assistantFieldHintOn: This article can be used by the AI assistant.
kb.assistantFieldHintOff: This article is hidden from the AI assistant.
